en.extract:
The black partridge (Melanoperdix niger), also known as the black wood partridge, is a small (up to 27 cm long) partridge with a thick bill, grey legs and dark brown iris. It is the only member of the monotypic genus Melanoperdix.
The black partridge is sexually dimorphic. The male has entirely glossy black plumage and a black bill, while the female is generally a chestnut-brown bird with a whitish throat and belly and a dark horn-colored bill. The female is smaller than the male.
The black partridge occurs in lowland rainforests of Peninsular Malaysia, Borneo and Sumatra in southeast Asia. It was formerly found but is long extinct on Singapore. The female usually lays five to six white eggs.
Due to ongoing habitat loss, small population size and overhunting in some areas, the black partridge is evaluated as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species. It is listed on Appendix III of CITES in Malaysia.


Subspecies
The black partridge has two recognized subspecies:

M. n. borneensis (Rothschild, 1917)
M. n. niger (Vigors, 1829)


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet

The '''black partridge''' ('''''Melanoperdix niger'''''), also known as the '''black wood partridge''', is a small (up to 27 cm long) [[partridge]] with a thick [[Beak|bill]], grey legs and dark brown [[Iris (anatomy)|iris]]. It is the only member of the monotypic genus ''Melanoperdix''.

The black partridge is [[Sexual dimorphism|sexually dimorphic]]. The male has entirely glossy black plumage and a black bill, while the female is generally a chestnut-brown bird with a whitish throat and belly and a dark horn-colored bill. The female is smaller than the male.

The black partridge occurs in lowland rainforests of [[Peninsular Malaysia]], [[Borneo]] and [[Sumatra]] in [[southeast Asia]]. It was formerly found but is long extinct on [[Singapore]]. The female usually lays five to six white eggs.

Due to ongoing [[habitat destruction|habitat loss]], small population size and [[overhunting]] in some areas, the black partridge is evaluated as [[Vulnerable species|Vulnerable]] on the [[IUCN Red List]] of Threatened Species. It is listed on Appendix III of [[CITES]] in [[Malaysia]].

== Subspecies ==
The black partridge has two recognized subspecies:<ref>{{Cite journal |title=IOC World Bird List 13.1 |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref |language=en |doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.13.1|doi-access=free |journal = IOC World Bird List}}</ref>

* ''M. n. borneensis'' <small>(Rothschild, 1917)</small>
* ''M. n. niger'' <small>(Vigors, 1829)</small>

zh.extract:
黑鶉（學名：Melanoperdix niger）是一種細小的鷓鴣。是黑鶉屬的唯一種。其种加词“niger”意为“黑色的”。


形态
黑鶉長27厘米，喙厚，腳呈灰色，瞳孔深褐色。
黑鶉是兩性異形的。雄鳥全身及喙都是黑色的；雌鳥一般是栗褐色，喉嚨白色，喙深色。雌鳥較雄鳥細小。
黑鶉分佈在馬來西亞、汶萊及蘇門答臘的低地雨林中。牠們曾於新加坡出沒，但咸信已在當地絕跡。雌鳥一般會生5-6隻蛋，蛋呈白色。
由於失去棲息地、數量稀少及過度捕獵，黑鶉被世界自然保護聯盟列為易危，且受到《瀕危野生動植物種國際貿易公約》附錄三的保護。
